aids.title: Probing Order: LQD
aids.content: Linear -> Quadratic -> Double. LQD also reminds you of increasing sophistication: Linear is simplest (step 1), Quadratic uses squares (step i^2), Double uses a second hash function (most complex but best distribution).
aids.forRemembering: The three open addressing probing techniques in order of complexity

aids.title: Static Hashing as a Parking Lot
aids.content: A static hash table is like a parking lot with a fixed number of spaces. The hash function is like your assigned parking spot number. If your spot is taken (collision), linear probing means you drive to the next spot, quadratic probing means you skip ahead by increasing distances, and chaining means you double-park behind the car in your spot.
aids.forRemembering: How static hashing and its collision resolution techniques work

aids.title: Clustering Problems: Primary vs Secondary
aids.content: Primary clustering (linear probing): long runs of occupied slots form because all keys that hash near a cluster join and extend it. Secondary clustering (quadratic probing): keys that hash to the same slot follow the same probe sequence. Double hashing avoids both.
aids.forRemembering: Why different probing methods have different clustering behaviors
